label: Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs

node.label: Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs
node.definition: Bayesian networks, Markov random fields, and factor graphs factorize a joint distribution into local relationships that support modular probabilistic inference.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs is to connect Graph theory, Bayesian & approximate inference to downstream capabilities including Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning.
examples: substrate for Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Graph theory, Bayesian & approximate inference.

dependencies.rationale: Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Graph theory.
Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Bayesian & approximate inference.
Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing.
